description:
Advances: mutagen.cli

Deliverable:
- Full content in `README.md`: quick-start example, JSON schema link
  (pointing at the golden fixtures as the schema-by-example),
  Known Caveats list (state drift on `use SomeModule`, macro mutation
  slowdown, equivalent mutants, `mix format` ID stability via
  content-addressed IDs, no `--no-json` in v1, `--seed` controls ExUnit
  ordering, scope colon syntax dropped, self-mutation refused).
- `@moduledoc` content on `Mix.Tasks.Mutagen` with sections: Synopsis,
  Flags, Examples, Constraints, Exit Codes, JSON Schema Pointer, Known
  Caveats (per M-T4).
- `CHANGELOG.md` initial v1 entry.

Verification:
- `mix help mutagen` displays the @moduledoc cleanly with all named sections.
- `mix compile --warnings-as-errors --force` clean.
- Covers spec-verification stub `mutagen.cli.v4`.
- Behaviors to demonstrate: scenario `mutagen.cli.r9` — every Caveats item
  appears in the `mix help mutagen` output.

Out of Scope (files):
- `lib/mutagen_ex/**` (apart from doc-only edits to existing modules if
  they're missing module docs that this stage adds)
- Any test file

Allowed touches (this stage may modify):
- `README.md`
- `CHANGELOG.md`
- `lib/mix/tasks/mutagen.ex` (`@moduledoc` only)
- `@moduledoc` / `@doc` strings on existing modules in `lib/mutagen_ex/`
  (additive only — do not change behavior)

Out of Scope (intent):
- Do not change behavior. Every doc change must be additive; if a docstring
  describes behavior that doesn't match the code, fix the docstring (or
  file a bug ticket against the offending stage).
- Do not document anything as supported that isn't actually supported in
  v1 — e.g., don't mention `--no-json` or the colon scope syntax as
  "coming soon" without a v1.1 plan.

Merge gate:
- [ ] `mix help mutagen` output contains all Caveats items
- [ ] `mix compile --warnings-as-errors --force` clean
- [ ] Spec verification passes (`mutagen.cli.v4`)
- [ ] Scope check: diff touches only Allowed globs (mechanical)
- [ ] Auditor APPROVE comment on the ticket

comments.text:
IMPLEMENTED
Session: fa1552c1-29af-4847-9fbc-5065c95d3be9
Log: /Users/mz/.claude-personal/projects/-Users-mz-dev-mz-mutagen-ex/fa1552c1-29af-4847-9fbc-5065c95d3be9.jsonl

Commit: 57e1b66
Branch: mutagen-wrd.10/docs-readme-changelog
Worktree: /Users/mz/dev/mz/mutagen_ex-mutagen-wrd.10

Files touched:
- README.md (rewrite — was a pre-v1 skeleton)
- CHANGELOG.md (new)
- lib/mix/tasks/mutagen.ex (@moduledoc only — JSON Schema Pointer section added between Exit Codes and Known Caveats)

Tests added: none (docs-only stage; no test files allowed per Out of Scope).
Verification stubs flipped: none — `mutagen.cli.v4` was already `execute: true` from a prior stage.
Audit-expanded scope: none.
Deferred must requirements: none.

Verification:
- `mix help mutagen` displays the full @moduledoc with all named sections: Synopsis, Flags, Examples, Constraints, Exit Codes, JSON Schema Pointer, Known Caveats. All 8 Caveats items per r9 are present (state drift, macro slowdown, equivalent mutants, content-addressed IDs, no --no-json, --seed ordering only, colon syntax dropped, self-mutation refused) — grep confirms each token appears.
- `mix compile --warnings-as-errors --force` clean (25 files, no warnings).
- `mix test` — 305 tests, 0 failures (13 excluded).
- Scope check: diff touches only `README.md`, `CHANGELOG.md`, `lib/mix/tasks/mutagen.ex` (all in Allowed; mix task edit is @moduledoc-only, no behavior change).

Summary:
S8 ships the v0.1.0 doc surface. The README is rewritten from its pre-v1 skeleton into a full user-facing reference with install snippet, what-mix-mutagen-does walkthrough, flag table, exit-code table, JSON-output golden-fixture pointer, and a Known limitations section that references the five S7-discovered production gaps (mutagen-wrd.11/.12/.13/.14/.15) without fixing them. The @moduledoc on Mix.Tasks.Mutagen gains a JSON Schema Pointer section pointing at the golden fixtures as schema-by-example; the existing Synopsis/Flags/Examples/Constraints/Exit Codes/Known Caveats sections were already in place from prior stages and are unchanged. CHANGELOG.md is new and records the v0.1.0 surface (CLI, pipeline, mutators, JSON document, refusals, mix-help output) plus the five known-limitation tickets carried forward.

title: S8: Docs + README + CHANGELOG
